messages.content: Hi, can you please improve this sentence: I’m seeking advice on a termination of an employee in my team whether it should be with or without cause. Our COO, Martine Tollefson is currently out of country so she asked me to reach out to you before taking any actions. I left you a voice message at 403-921-9855 as well this morning. would you mind returning my call at your earliest convenience?

I am currently seeking advice on whether to terminate an employee on my team with or without cause. However, our COO, Martine Tollefson, is currently out of the country, so she requested that I reach out to you for guidance before proceeding. I have also left a voice message for you this morning at 403-921-9855. Would you kindly return my call at your earliest convenience?

Hi there, can you please improve the following sentence: I just received a phone call from Roland. He is trying to add 2346515 AB to his CRA portal (?) but apparently he can’t since he is not the primary contact. He seems to think that MEP is the primary contact for 234. I understand that MEP is the registered office but not sure if this is the same thing as the primary contact for CRA. Roland would like Shawna and himself to be the primary contacts so that they can add 234 to the CRA portal if this makes sense to you. Also, he mentioned that PWC is handling 234, not Nuvero and they would like to send the outstanding items mentioned in the letter to sent to CRA today. Sounded like they can be sent through this ‘portal’? He highlighted ‘today’ multiple time – I do not know the realistic timeline for this type of request but assistance would be appreciated.

I recently received a phone call from Roland, who is attempting to add 2346515 AB to his CRA portal. However, he is encountering difficulties since he is not listed as the primary contact. He seems to believe that MEP is the primary contact for 234, but I am unsure if MEP being the registered office is equivalent to being the primary contact for CRA. Roland wishes for Shawna and himself to be designated as the primary contacts to be able to add 234 to the CRA portal, if that makes sense. Additionally, he mentioned that PWC is handling 234, not Nuvero, and they would like to send the outstanding items mentioned in the letter to CRA today. It appears that they can be sent through this 'portal.' Roland emphasized the urgency by mentioning 'today' multiple times, and though I am unsure of the realistic timeline for this request, any assistance would be greatly appreciated.
make it little less formal please

I just got a call from Roland. He's trying to add 2346515 AB to his CRA portal but it seems like he can't do it because he's not the main contact. He thinks MEP is the main contact for 234, but I'm not sure if that's the same as the primary contact for CRA. Roland and Shawna want to be the main contacts so they can add 234 to the CRA portal, if you catch my drift. Oh, and he also mentioned that PWC is handling 234, not Nuvero. They need to send some stuff mentioned in the letter to CRA today, and they're wondering if they can do it through this 'portal' thingy. Roland is really stressing about getting it done 'today' – I don't know how doable that is, but any help would be awesome.
please improve this sentence: Both our CEO and COO are unavailable at this time, so I’m provide the answer to the best of my knowledge.
Since both our CEO and COO are currently unavailable, I will do my best to provide an answer based on my knowledge.

Chris advised me after your last meeting with him on the 13th that the 2023 financials is about to be finalized. If that is the case, can you kindly provide me with the following items for Alma so that we could move forward with the underwriting?

After your last meeting with Chris on the 13th, he mentioned that the finalization of the 2023 financials is imminent. If that is indeed the case, could you please provide me with the following items for Alma, as we need them to proceed with the underwriting process?

I wanted to let you know that the underwriter received the medical reports from Dr. Friggstad on September 20th and 25th, and they are currently being reviewed. This left us with the question about the ownership of Nexus Holdings. Can you please confirm if you and Ken are the sole owners? If there are other owners, please provide their names and respective ownership percentages.

I wanted to inform you that the underwriter has received the medical reports from Dr. Friggstad on September 20th and 25th, and they are currently undergoing review. This has raised a question regarding the ownership of Nexus Holdings. Can you please confirm if you and Ken are the only owners, or if there are additional owners involved? If there are indeed other owners, kindly provide their names and the respective percentage of ownership they hold.

I was explained that being the registered office of a corporation is not equivalent to being the primary contact for CRA. Only directors can be the primary contacts for the CRA My Business Account and the whole CRA online portal system works based on social insurance numbers. The reasons you weren’t able to access in the first place would probably because CRA doesn’t have a SIN pre-assigned to your and Shawna’s names. When a corporation is incorporated, the director’s SIN is not provided (the forms doesn’t even have a space for it). So, CRA might know that you are the directors but without SIN they cannot identify that you are the primary contact for the portal purposes. Apparently the name was enough in the past but with the new My Business Account system we have to make sure the director’s SIN is also tied into the CRA system. 

I was advised that the information can also be faxed to the CRA agent if this online access cannot be set up quickly and PWC should be able to address this matter for you.

I understand now that being the registered office of a corporation does not automatically mean being the primary contact for CRA. Only directors can be designated as the primary contacts for the CRA My Business Account, and the entire CRA online portal system operates based on social insurance numbers. The reason you weren't able to access it initially may be because CRA doesn't have your and Shawna's SINs pre-assigned to your names. When a corporation is incorporated, the director's SIN is not provided, as the forms do not have a space for it. Therefore, CRA may recognize you as directors, but without the SIN, they cannot identify you as the primary contacts for portal purposes. It seems that simply providing the name was sufficient in the past, but with the new My Business Account system, it is necessary to ensure that the director's SIN is also linked to the CRA system.

I was advised that if setting up online access takes too long, the information can also be faxed to the CRA agent. PWC should be able to assist you in addressing this matter promptly.
PWC should be able to assist you in addressing this matter promptly and setting you and Shawna as the primary contacts.
PWC should be able to promptly assist you in addressing this matter and ensuring that you and Shawna are designated as the primary contacts.
When a corporation is incorporated, the director’s SIN is not provided, as it is not a requirement and also the forms do not have a space for it.
When a corporation is incorporated, it is not a requirement to provide the director's SIN, and the forms do not include a space to fill in the SIN information.

The cash value of Manulife policy as of June 30, 2023 is $3,105,870.25. I apologize for the delay – Manulife is experiencing a backlog, so I had to put some pressures on certain people to obtain this for your. Hope this is not too late for your filing.

The cash value of the Manulife policy as of June 30, 2023 is $3,105,870.25. I apologize for the delay in obtaining this information. Manulife has been experiencing a backlog, and I had to apply pressure to certain individuals to secure it for you. I hope this is not too late for your filing.

please improve the following sentence: If you remember, Sheri did not want to show the numbers because of Rahim’s agent friend. I think we will need to send him the quote comparison for Almas. Without the financial information I cannot finish the underwriting. And technically the numbers should have been shared a long time ago.

If you recall, Sheri was hesitant to share the numbers due to Rahim's connection with an agent friend. I believe it is necessary to send him the quote comparison for Almas. Without the financial information, I am unable to complete the underwriting process. Technically, the numbers should have been shared a long time ago.

Just wanted to let you know that Violet requested a meeting with myself and Paul this morning. Paul is the other management member attended yesterday’s meeting as witness. 

Bottom line is that she wanted to express
•	She’s been having a rough year (couple of death in her family which we already knew).
•	She cares about our clients, company and she loves what she does.
•	She knows that she’s not good at asking for help when she should, but getting better at it. 
•	She understands that we have a decision to make but wanted to let us know where she’s coming from.

Martine and I are aware of her life events this year, and accommodated her need very generously. Violet’s performance is impacted significantly in a negative way when something happens in her life. I already had a conversation with her about this and provided advice and guidance (including reminding her we have the employee benefit offering free counseling), but it did not get better and she acknowledged that. 

She was emotional and cried during this meeting. I indicated that we appreciate her wanting to express her position and thoughts but we ultimately have a business to run (on behalf of Sheri) and this is not a small mistake that could be overlooked. I think she feels that the termination might be on its way to her. My decision remains unchanged (termination) as long as Sheri and Martine supports my decision. 

We will wait for your recommendation.

I wanted to inform you that Violet requested a meeting with myself and Paul this morning. Paul, who was present as a witness at yesterday's meeting, is another member of the management team. 

In summary, during the meeting, Violet conveyed the following points:
- She has been going through a difficult year, including the loss of a few family members, which we were already aware of.
- She genuinely cares about our clients, the company, and enjoys her work.
- Violet recognized that she struggles with asking for help when needed, but she is working on improving in that area.
- She understands that we have a decision to make regarding her situation but wanted to share her perspective.

Both Martine and I were already aware of the life events that Violet has experienced this year, and we have made generous accommodations to support her. However, it is important to note that Violet's performance is significantly impacted in a negative way when she faces personal challenges. I have already had a conversation with her about this and provided guidance, including reminding her about the employee benefit offering free counseling, but the situation did not improve as expected, as she acknowledged.

During the meeting, Violet became emotional and shed tears. I expressed our appreciation for her willingness to share her thoughts and position, but I emphasized that ultimately, we have a business to run on behalf of Sheri, and this is not a minor mistake that can be overlooked. It seems that she may be concerned that termination might be the outcome. My decision regarding termination remains unchanged as long as Sheri and Martine are supportive of it.

Now, we await your recommendation on how to proceed.

Would it be safe for me to assume that you are meaning to use the IA non-registered investment to pay off the loan balance? If that is the case, the process is rather simple. One page form needs to be completed by you and Deborah to take the funds out of the non-registered accounts and pay down or pay off completely the loan balance. Please bear in mind that this will trigger a capital gain from cashing part of your non-registered investment and you will no longer benefit from the interest deduction going forward. May I ask why you would like to pay off the loan?

If my understanding is correct, you are considering using the IA non-registered investment to pay off the loan balance. If that is the case, the process is relatively straightforward. A one-page form needs to be completed by you and Deborah in order to withdraw funds from the non-registered accounts and use them to either partially or fully pay off the loan balance. It is important to note that this action will result in a capital gain from liquidating a portion of your non-registered investment and you will no longer be eligible for the interest deduction in the future. Before proceeding, may I inquire about your reasons for wanting to pay off the loan?

We had worked with a particular accountant (Warren Krepps) from that firm in the past for other clients’ case but I do not recall that this person was assigned to your file in any ways. Does Warren Krepps sound more familiar to you by any chance?

In the past, we have collaborated with a specific accountant named Warren Krepps from that firm for other clients' cases. However, I cannot recall if this person was assigned to your file in any capacity. Does the name Warren Krepps ring a bell or sound familiar to you in any way?

I’m glad to hear that you remember me! I’d be more than happy to help you once the sale of your old house has been completed. 

Please allow me few days to work on your other inquiries – I will get back to you as soon as I can.

It's great to hear that you remember me! I will be more than happy to assist you once the sale of your old house has been finalized. Please allow me a few days to address your other inquiries, and I will reach out to you as soon as possible with the information you need.

Please find attached spreadsheet comparing Manulife and IA’s quotes. This comparison contains so much information in one page, I think it would be better for us to have a review meeting to go over each component.

I have received the attached spreadsheet comparing the quotes from Manulife and IA. It appears to contain comprehensive information on a single page. In order to effectively review each component, I believe it would be more beneficial for us to schedule a meeting dedicated to this discussion. This will allow us to thoroughly go through the details and address any questions or concerns that may arise.
It contains lot of information on a single page. I think it would be beneficial for us to schedule a meeting and go through the details together and address any questions or concerns that may arise.

The attached spreadsheet contains a significant amount of information compiled on a single page. To ensure a comprehensive understanding and to address any questions or concerns, I believe it would be highly beneficial for us to schedule a meeting where we can go through the details together. This will allow us to thoroughly discuss and analyze the information provided.
please make it little shorter
The attached spreadsheet contains extensive information on a single page. To ensure clarity and address any questions or concerns, let's schedule a meeting to review the details together.
too short

Given the amount of information presented on the attached spreadsheet, it would be beneficial for us to schedule a meeting. This way, we can go through the details together, address any questions or concerns, and ensure a thorough understanding.
Would the morning of next Tuesday (3rd) or Wednesday (4th) work for both of you?
Would the morning of either Tuesday (3rd) or Wednesday (4th) work for both of you to schedule a meeting?

Just wanted to let you know that we received the attached notice from IA as we do each year. Violet is currently working on correcting your address to BC. 

I understand that you haven’t made any deposits in the past couple of years but as I mentioned in my previous email your policy currently has a deposit room of $147K. Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns.

I wanted to inform you that we have received the attached notice from IA, which we receive annually. Violet is currently working on updating your address to BC as requested. 

I understand that no deposits have been made in the past couple of years. However, I would like to remind you that your policy currently has a deposit room of $147K. If you have any questions or concerns regarding this information, please do not hesitate to let me know.

I understand that no deposits have been made in the past couple of years. However, as I mentioned in my previous email, your policy currently has a deposit room of $147K if you would like to take advantage of tax-sheltered growth

I understand that there have been no deposits made in the past couple of years. However, I would like to remind you that your policy currently has a deposit room of $147K available. If you are interested in taking advantage of tax-sheltered growth, please let me know, and we can discuss further options.
please rephrase.

I acknowledge that no deposits have been made in recent years. Nevertheless, I want to remind you that your policy still holds a deposit room of $147K, providing you with the opportunity to benefit from tax-sheltered growth. If you are interested in exploring this option or have any concerns or questions, please feel free to inform me.
less stiff please

I understand that you haven't made any deposits in the past couple of years. However, I wanted to bring to your attention that your policy currently has a deposit room of $147K available. This means you have the opportunity to take advantage of tax-sheltered growth if you're interested. If you have any questions or concerns, please don't hesitate to let me know.
please incorporate that I already mentioned the deposit room

This means you can still benefit from tax-sheltered growth if you choose to do so. If you have any questions or concerns regarding this, please feel free to let me know.
less condescending please

I understand that you may already be familiar with this information, but I wanted to kindly remind you that your policy has a deposit room of $147K available, even though no deposits have been made recently. You still have the option
